data.title: First Citizens BancShares declared dividends for common and preferred stock on July 25, 2025, to be paid on September 15 to holders of record by August 29.
data.description: On July 25, 2025, in Raleigh, North Carolina, First Citizens BancShares, Inc. (Nasdaq: FCNCA) announced dividend declarations for its common and preferred stock. The dividends will be paid on September 15, 2025, to shareholders of record as of August 29.

data.title: Lear Corp. (NYSE: LEA) delivered $6.0 billion in revenue in Q2 2025 - a global automotive leader in Seating and E-Systems.
data.description: Southfield, Michigan - In a recent report for the second quarter of 2025, Lear Corporation, a prominent player in Seating and E-Systems within the automotive technology sector, revealed key financial updates. During this period, the company achieved a significant milestone by generating $6.0 billion in revenue. Lear Corporation continues to solidify its position as a global leader in the industry, showcasing robust performance in the latest quarter.

data.title: OneMain Holdings reports strong Q2 2025 results: Diluted EPS $1.40, C&I adjusted EPS $1.45, receivables $25.2B, dividend $1.04.
data.description: In the second quarter of 2025, OneMain Holdings, Inc. (NYSE: OMF) reported a diluted EPS of $1.40 and an adjusted diluted EPS for C&I of $1.45. The company managed receivables worth $25.2 billion during this period. Additionally, they declared a quarterly dividend of $1.04 per share. Based in New York, OneMain Holdings, Inc. is a prominent provider to nonprime consumers.

data.title: ALLETE board declares dividend of 73 cents per share, equivalent to $2.92 annually, payable Sept 1 to shareholders. Headquarters in Duluth, Minnesota.
data.description: In Duluth, Minnesota, ALLETE, Inc. (NYSE: ALE) announced a quarterly dividend of 73 cents per common stock share, totaling $2.92 annually, staying consistent with the previous quarter. Shareholders as of August 15, 2025, will receive the dividend on September 1. ALLETE, Inc., a energy company based in Duluth, also provides electric utilities services.

data.title: Sen. Elizabeth Warren criticizes Dems backing crypto bills, citing industry influence over public interest.
data.description: Senator Elizabeth Warren of Massachusetts criticized Democratic lawmakers for supporting cryptocurrency bills in recent endorsements. She expressed her concerns during an interview with Vanity Fair, highlighting the trend of prioritizing industry interests over the needs of the public for a fair economy and affordable living. The senior lawmaker emphasized the influence of money in Washington, stating that the town is known for favoring industry influence. Warren mentioned her support for robust cryptocurrency legislation but cautioned against rushing through bills that she believes may not be well-crafted.

data.title: First Citizens BancShares (Nasdaq: FCNCA) announces Q2 2025 earnings; Chairman and CEO pleased with solid financial results.
data.description: In Raleigh, North Carolina, on July 25, 2025, First Citizens BancShares, Inc. (Nasdaq: FCNCA) released its earnings report for the second quarter of 2025. The Chairman and CEO, Frank B. Holding, Jr., expressed satisfaction with the performance, stating that the company achieved strong financial results during this period. The specific earnings and revenue figures were not disclosed, but Holding emphasized that the team's efforts were successful in generating positive outcomes for the quarter.

data.title: Weekly recap of 14 key financial press releases, featuring PayPal World launch and Q2 earnings report.
data.description: This week, key financial announcements were highlighted by PR Newswire, such as the debut of PayPal World and a summary of the second-quarter earnings. The updates were shared from New York on July 25, 2025, underscoring the challenges in sifting through numerous weekly press releases. PayPal World's launch aimed to revolutionize online transactions, while the recap of second-quarter earnings shed light on the financial performance of various entities. These releases provided valuable insights into the financial landscape, demonstrating the dynamic nature of the market.

data.title: Donanemab recommended by CHMP for early symptomatic Alzheimer's disease. Final EU decision pending. Eli Lilly: NYSE: LLY.
data.description: Eli Lilly and Company (NYSE: LLY) has disclosed that the European Medicines Agency's (EMA) Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use has finalized its review of donanemab. The decision is now in the hands of the European Commission for the ultimate regulatory approval. This update comes from Indianapolis on July 25, 2025. The specifics of the decision and next steps will be determined as the regulatory process continues.

data.title: Weekly recap of 12 healthcare press releases includes Sanofi's $1.6B acquisition and a breakthrough nerve treatment.
data.description: This week's notable healthcare press releases featured on PR Newswire include Sanofi's recent $1.6 billion acquisition and a pioneering surgical nerve treatment designed for veterans. The updates were released on July 25, 2025, in New York, showcasing the ever-evolving landscape of the healthcare industry. The acquisition deal by Sanofi amounts to a significant sum of $1.6 billion, indicating a strategic move by the company to expand its portfolio or strengthen its market presence. On the other hand, the innovative nerve treatment tailored for veterans highlights the continual advancements in medical technologies and treatments to address specific healthcare needs. This latest information underscores the diverse and impactful developments taking place within the healthcare sector, aiming to enhance services and improve patient outcomes.

data.title: Plasmatreat introduces HydroPlasma® for surface cleaning: eco-friendly solution for glass and metal, with ultra-precise effects.
data.description: Germany's Plasmatreat GmbH, a top player in atmospheric plasma technology, recently introduced HydroPlasma®, a new environmentally friendly solution for meticulously cleaning glass and metal surfaces. This innovation aims to offer exceptional precision and efficiency in cleaning processes, addressing the growing demand for sustainable solutions. The unveiling of HydroPlasma® marks a significant milestone for Plasmatreat GmbH, reinforcing its position as a frontrunner in the industry. The product is expected to revolutionize the sector with its cutting-edge technology and sustainable approach.

data.title: LandGate® reports Q2 2025 U.S. Data Center Development Summary shows growth in AI-powered computing sector. Important insights for stakeholders.
data.description: On July 25, 2025, LandGate® published its report on U.S. Data Center Development for the second quarter of 2025, indicating ongoing progress and expansion within the data center industry in response to increasing need for AI-driven computing. The report serves as a valuable resource for developers and investors, presenting key statistics and trends in the sector.

data.title: Inception Growth Acquisition Limited postpones special meeting to August 8, 2025, regarding proposed business combination with AgileAlgo Holdings Ltd.
data.description: On July 25, 2025, Inception Growth Acquisition Limited, a blank check company, announced the postponement of its special meeting to August 8, 2025. The meeting, which did not conduct any business, was adjourned concerning all proposals outlined in the definitive proxy statement filed with the SEC on May 27, 2025, along with the supplement released on June 26, 2025. This includes the vote on the potential business merger with AgileAlgo Holdings Ltd.

data.title: Volkswagen loses $1.5 billion in profit due to Trump tariffs, revises yearly forecast downward.
data.description: According to recent reports, a prominent German car manufacturer disclosed that import tariffs imposed by the United States had a detrimental impact on its profits, resulting in a reduction of approximately $1.5 billion during the initial six months of the year. Consequently, the company has revised its projections for the entire year due to this significant financial setback.

data.title: Arizona woman gets 8.5 years for aiding North Korea hackers in targeting 300 US crypto and tech firms, making $17M.
data.description: In the state of Arizona, a woman has been given a sentence of 8.5 years for her involvement in assisting North Korean hackers in breaching more than 300 American cryptocurrency and technology companies. The illegal activities conducted by the hackers resulted in a total of $17 million in illicit profits.

data.title: $9.7B OG Bitcoin moved by Galaxy, additional $1.1B to exchanges. Analysts foresee minimal market impact.
data.description: Crypto market analysts predict that the recent $9.7 billion transaction in blockchain technology could be assimilated by the market without causing any major impact.
